source_pr.title: executor: fix wrong result when index join on union scan.
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

If outer rows of index lookup join is larger than one batch, result of index lookup join is wrong.

### What is changed and how it works?

Do not modify Plan of dataReaderBuilder directly, because it would impact next batch of outer rows, as well as other concurrent inner workers. Instead, build a local child builder to store the child plan.

Also, modify `buildUnionScanFromReader` to make it multi-threaded safe.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Side effects

N/A

Related changes

N/A

symptoms: address comment and avoid data race
failure_signature: address comment and avoid data race
root_cause_explanation: The failure was due to a concurrency bug in index lookup join with union scan: shared mutable state (e.g., dataReaderBuilder.Plan / union scan construction) could be modified concurrently across batches/workers, leading to wrong results or data races. This PR fixes it by using a local child builder per call and making buildUnionScanFromReader safe for concurrent inner workers, with a regression test.
fix_pattern: Avoid shared mutable builder state in concurrent IndexLookupJoin inner workers; build per-call child builders and make union scan construction thread-safe.

evidence: evidence: address comment and avoid data race
